docs.title: Silicon Dust
docs.text:
A game by Valentin Lageard and Hugo Lavaill.

Note to the reader : this system is a work in progress, there are large missing pieces, incoherent mechanics and denominations, ... Use it at your own peril !

Silicon Dust is an investigation PBTA roleplaying game set in a post mind digitisation future. It non-exhaustively features:

  • Narratively rich archetypes and expertises for players.
  • Investigation system with threads and clues for the MC.
  • Full shell customization and reshelling rules.
  • Mind forking rules but beware, one of you may get rogue.
  • A faction system with favors, gigs and retaliation.
  • Pregenerated gear and assets, as well as full customization in option.

The setting of Silicon Dust is a future where humanity became transhumanity at the crisscross of mind uploading technology, brain-computer interface, body enhancement and space colonization. Its setting is inspired from Altered Carbon and Eclipse Phase.

docs.title: Transhumanity
docs.text:
In Silicon Dust, transhumans have a cortical stack usually located at the back of their neck, a device perpetually scanning and storing biophysical changes in the brain. Cortical stack, in effect, scan the brain and stores the data. Such data is called a topology and is a faithful backup of what makes one's personality and identity : their memories, preferences, beliefs, emotions, skills, ... But topologies may be edited through psychosurgery essentially rewriting the minds of its patients.

In Silicon Dust, death is not much a threat. If your body were to die, your cortical stack or a virtual backup of your topology could be retrieved and uploaded in a new body. Bodies as a commodity are mere shells. Changing shells (because of death or otherwise) is called reshelling. Shells are diverse, they can be biological or synthetic, they can be humanoid or not. Shells are specialised by their augs, short for augmentations, which give various enhancements for the being in the shell : faster cognition, superhuman reflexes and strength, ... Augs are a necessity in the future because skill alone cannot rival with cutting edge technology. But augs and shells are costly and as such, are not well distributed amongst the population. The rich and powerful have immensely more efficient shells and augs than the poor and oppressed.

In Silicon Dust, all information systems and their various interactions are called the Grid, the successor of our modern day internet. The Grid is interacted with using a brain-computer interface called a Grid interface. Grid interface allow to connect perceptions and thoughts and information system technology. It takes the form of augmented reality (AR) when a layer of multisensory holograms are added on top of normal perceptions. It may also take the form of simulspaces when the perceived environment is entirely simulated. Habitats and objects are connected to the Grid by default allowing people to interact with them. A local layer of interfaces to habitats utilities and objects is called a mesh. Mesh experiences varies from person to person and place to place : advertising may be oppressive, art and style may be chaotic or streamlined and unified, interactions may be open or restrictive, ...

In Silicon Dust, transhumans explored the solar system where they setup artificial habitats from floating aerostats in Venus atmosphere to 0g stations in orbit around Jupiter to nanotech domes on Mars surface. Transhumans also found ancient alien gates in the solar system allowing to travel instantaneously to other systems in the galaxy. These gates are a lottery, you may find an alien world filled with interesting and valuable exobiologies, or a black hole, sucking you in its relativistic time trap.

docs.title: Lexicon
docs.text: AI An Artificial Intelligence. This term is generally used to refer to simple artificial intelligences with specific purposes. AGI An Artificial General Intelligence. This refers to artificial beings capable of developing their own objectives and intentions and capable of solving all problems solvable by transhumans. AGIs are usually illegal, rejected or heavily monitored in most societies as they are feared to become singularities. Angel A personal assistant AI embedded in your MPU. Most people have angels since childhood and develop a particular relationship with them. They can interface with you HUD and talk to you or even manifest visually in the Mesh. Angels are devoted and useful assistants that can use your inserts to navigate the Grid, helping you to access information, synthesize it, navigate easily, ... Angels are commonly a pseudofork of their user or of someone close, but they may be entirely artificial intelligence with programmable personality, the pseudofork of a famous person or even more exotic things... Aug An augmentation. A device or alteration integrated to your shell that provides you with a boon in efficiency or capability compared to baseline transhuman. Avatar An avatar is your perceptual representation for others in simulspaces. Basically, it's what they see, hear and feel when interacting with you in a virtual environment. Bioshell [...] Cortical stack A chip, usually located at the back of one's neck, constantly scanning your MPU and storing your topology. Daemon An Angel with softwares allowing to anonymize its user. Contrary to most of the population, your actions may not publicly available on the Grid. Daemon fakes public authentications, automatically tries to remove or falsify your traces in the local Mesh, ... Exhuman A transhuman that thinks ascension can be accessed only by rejecting what's left of humanity in transhumanity. While exhumans come in various shapes and the details of their ideology varies a lot, they all share this idea of abandonning the human drives and beliefs that we kept from our biological ancestry. Some of them push forking at its limits, creating competing communities of a single forked individual. Some interface with swarms of shells, others use nanotech swarms as a shell. Some study stolen alien artifacts to incorporate their technologies, others do even weirder stuffs in their hidden habitats. Exhumans are generally outcasts and some of them engage with terrorism to rebel against transhuman societies. Fork A fork is a copied instance of a topology, basically a perfect clone of your mind in another body. As a copy, it has the same memories, drives, beliefs, ... Of course, once the fork is running on an independent MPU, it progressively becomes someone else, diverging while their experiences slowly drift from the original. Having multiple forks of the same topology is generally considered a major crime in most societies. Geront An individual whose original ego was born before the Fall. Also means someone having lived for more than a century. Grid (The) The Grid describes the entirety of the information networks used by transhumans. The Grid is not a single network with fixed protocols but is instead a collection of networks with various properties, more or less connected between one another. Some parts of the Grid are heavily surveilled and controlled, while others are totally anonymous and free. Some parts are corporate encrypted internal servers, anonymous black markets, gamified VR social networks, etc... The Grid is not just an abstract construct, parts of it are embedded in the physical world and are called meshes. Some transhumans even live exclusively on the Grid as infoshells. Habitat Habitats are the constructions where transhumans live. Habitats are what protects transhumanity against the void of the space, solar radiation, toxic atmospheres and extreme temperatures. Humanity Humanity was the specie that preceded transhumanity, before topology technology was developed. Rare humans may still exist in small communities. Infoshell A virtual avatar of a transhuman. If its your main shell, it generally is run on a server in the Grid. Mesh A mesh describes the local ecosystem of habitat-embedded technologies and connected objects. The mesh is a physically spatialized part of the Grid composed of layers and layers of information and functionalities. Mesh are perceived through your HUD. MPU Mind Processing Unit. The part of your shell that loads your topology and runs it. It may be a biological brain, a silicon brain or even a server. Anything with enough computation power may be a MPU. This is the part scanned and stored by your cortical stack. Pseudofork An AI replicating a rough copy of you. Pseudoforks are commonly used as angels. Resurrection insurance [...] Shell A body that can be controlled from the inside by someone. Shells come in various shapes and functions. Simulspace An entirely simulated virtual environment. Singularity An AGI that developed beyond transhuman intelligence. Singularities are feared as they may be too powerful to be controlled and because it's extremely hard to tune their objectives parameters in a safe way. Synthshell A shell made of synthetic materials (plastic, metal, silicon, ...). Topology A scan of your mind : your identity, your memories, hopes, beliefs, drives and regrets. As data, your topology may be stored on data storage devices for later recall. To be active and dynamic, a topology must be run on a MPU. While topology is a technical term, there are other possible appellations : ego, topos, datamind, persona, ...

docs.title: Character creation
docs.text:
To create your character :

  1. Choose an Archetype.
    1. Fill in your Archetype specific mechanics and questions.
    2. Get your Archetype moves.
  2. Choose an Expertise.
    1. Get your Expertise moves.
    2. Get your Shell and fill in the details.
    3. Get your Gear and Assets.

  3. Choose a Background (depends) and answer its questions, answer also your archetype questions.

  4. Define your Angel.

  5. Get and choose your cred, Gear and assets according to your Archetype and Expertise.
  6. If you're not in your original body, you start with 1-trauma. If you have reshelled a lot before, you start with 2-trauma.
  7. Ask your Connection questions and develop your relationships.
  8. Choose a Name.

docs.title: Archetypes
docs.text:
Archetypes represent a mix of social position, motivations, identity and subgenre themes. At the character creation, every player chooses an archetype.

  • The Async awakened with mysterious psi abilities. Play the Async if you want to add metaphysical mysteries in your adventure, if you want to have weird powers beyond scientific comprehension and if you want to be on the run, tracked by organizations wanting to secure you as an asset, experiment on you or simply eliminate you.
  • The Construct is an artificial sentience in search of its purpose. Play the Construct if you want to understand what's left of humanity in transhumanity and what's your role in it.
  • The Exec is a corporate agent enacting the interests of a megacorporation. Play the Exec if you want to be deep in corpo drama, if you like the luxe and the privilege it gives and if you're ready to be ruthless to keep it.
  • The Exolog is fascinated by alien technology. Play the Exolog if you want to explore alien archeological site and study alien artifacts.
  • The Influencer builds its community by producing content, from high octane sports to sociocultural comment, from parasocial erotic dating to the elite lifestyle.
  • The Radical is an activist that fights for its cause. Play the Radical if you want to be a revolutionary defending its cause whatever the cost.
  • The Shadow is a freelancing operative working in the shadows. Play the Shadow if you want to be a competent freelancer and if you don't care who's paying you to do stuff.

docs.title: Expertise
docs.text:
Expertise represents skills. Every character chooses an expertise at the creation (except the Shadow who chooses 2) :

  • Cybernetics : The art of designing and integrating augs.
  • Design : The art of designing, modifying and repairing things.
  • Egoriding : The art of building and piloting drones.
  • Hacking : The art of penetrating and subverting information system.
  • Memegineering : The art of understanding and manipulating people and society.
  • Psychosurgery : The art of studying and operating directly into the psyche of people.
  • Specops : The art of military special operations.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
• Activist : You come from a community of radical activists. What are their cause and how did you ended embracing it ? Where are they based and how are they considered by the societies they're based in ? Are you still fighting for the cause ? If yes, what are your obligations to the cause ? If no, why did you quit ?
  • Construct : You are an artificial intelligence. Do you know who created you and do you know why ? What are your first memories as an AI ? Do you hide your true identity ? How did you build your personality ? If you know which entity created you, are they an organization, a corporation or a single individual ?
  • Corpo slave : You are owned by a corporation. Which one and how did you ended in their hands ? What is the nature of the bond between you and them and what do you do for them ? Are you still in their hands or did you manage to escape ? If you escaped, what do you risk if they catch you ?
  • Criminal : You're part or were part of a criminal organization. Who are they and what do they do ? How did you enter it ? What is/were your part in it ? If you're not part of them anymore, how did you leave and why do they resent and track you ?
  • Drifter : You were part of a nomadic group. They may be traders, pirates, scavengers, ... Why do they live nomadically and where do they usually travel ? What do they do and what was your part in it ? What parts of the system did you explore ? How would they react if you met them again ?
  • Immutable [Neuromorph] : You disabled neural plasticity in your value centers and deleted most or all pathways related to emotions. Did you do the operation ? Why ? What are your immutable values ? What are the suppressed emotions ?
  • Normal : You're a normal person. What makes you normal ? What is your normal life ? Has your normality ever been challenged, how did you react ?
  • Outer : You come from a fringe society outside the Consortium. It may be an weird anarchist community, an isolated religious cult, an habitat ruled by a tyrant, a utopian society, ... Where are they and how do they live ? What are their structural memes and their political organization ? What make them fringe ? What are their relations with other groups and what is/was your place in it ?
  • Precog [Neuromorph] : You wove many feedback pathways between unrelated parts of your brain, making you hypersensitive, intuitive, improvisational and somewhat unreliable. Did you do the operation ? Why ? How did it change your lived experience ?
  • Seraphim [Neuromorph] : You're a rare and experimental agglomerate of multiple forks of yourself. Illegal everywhere except in some outer habitats. Did you do the operation ? Why ? How do you hide ? How did it change your lived experience ?
  • Uplift [Neuromorph] : You were a "mindless" animal before being uplifted to sapience. Who uplifted you and do you know why ? What's your relation with them ? Do you think your sapience was anthropomorphic, how so ? Do you regret loosing your life as a non-sapient entity ? Did you succeed at integrating transhumanity ? What did you keep from your inner animal ?
  • Veteran : You were a soldier in one or several brutal war. What was these wars about and how were they brutal ? Were you on the winning or the loosing side ? How and when did you leave your former life and did you manage to reintegrate society ?
  • Young : You are young and have little experience of the transhuman world. What do you expect of the "real life" ? What do you want to be in this world ?

docs.title: Async
docs.text: Archetype introduction... Focus on the following: What are you playing? Why is it interesting?

docs.title: Async +Async
docs.text:
You awakened to be an async. Time flows chaotically for you, letting you unsure of its true ordering but capable of understanding the faintest threads of causality.

As an async, you have a unique stat, +Async, starting from +0. You can spend an improvement to get +1 Async (up to a maximum of +4).

Once each session or when you increase Async, roll +Async:

Success

The MC chooses an occurence and you answer the questions.

Partial

Choose an occurence and the MC answers the questions.

Miss

The MC chooses an occurence and answers the questions.

Occurences (cross out up to four if you don't want them to happen at all):

  • You awaken as an async. What happens ?
  • Someone else learns you're an async ? Who ?
  • Your async capacities are affected. By what/who ? How ?
  • You change in a radical way. How so ? Why ?
  • You lose something or someone important. What ? Why ?
  • Someone who has been looking for you finds you. Who ?
  • You meet another async. Who ? What happens ?
  • You are experimented upon ? By whom ? How ? Take 1 trauma.
  • Someone understood the truth of your existence ? Who ? How do they react ?
  • You understand the nature of the async field. How ?
  • You understand the origin of your capabilities. How ?
  • ________________________________________________
  • ________________________________________________

Initially, an occurence isn't placed in your timeline, you're unsure of it's in the past, the present or the future. At any moment, you can place an occurence on your timeline by stating when it happened relative to now and other already placed occurences. When you do so, the Mandela effect and Time compression moves can be triggered if you have them.

When at least 10 occurences have been placed in your timeline, whenever you want, choose 1:

  • You find a way to propagate in the async field, leaving your shell, locality and present altogether. Make a new character.
  • You always knew that at some point your asyc abilities would leave you and you would be left here and now. You loose the Async archetype and all its moves. Make a new character or choose a new archetype for this one.
  • You realize that your async abilities were given to you to achieve a grand purpose. When you achieve it, you loose the Async archetype and all its moves. Make a new character or choose a new archetype for this one.
  • One of your vision turns out false, creating a paradox that threatens reality itself. The MC creates a Paradox threat. If it fills out completely, the universe will just stop existing. The clock is ticking...
  • Upon your sacrifice, you can rewrite an important part of history or force something important to happen in the future. What will your sacrifice be for ?

docs.title: Specimen
docs.text: Asyncs are rare and their powers misunderstood. Various interests would capture and study you if they knew about your capacities.

docs.title: Asynchronous vision +Async
docs.text:
When you desynchronise yourself from here and now, ask something and roll +Async.

Success

MC gives you a clear perception and you know if it's in the past, the present or the future.

Partial

MC gives you impressions and you don't know when it's happening.

Miss

MC gives you a horrific vision, prepare for the worst.

The vision necessarily has happened, is happening or will happen.

docs.title: Butterfly effect +Async
docs.text:
When you feel the threads of causality and want something to happen, ask the MC what should you do to make it happen and roll +Async.

Success

The MC tells you of something relatively simple to do to make it happen.

Partial

The MC tells you of something complex and 2 unintended side-effects. If it happens, throw a coin to see which happens.

Miss

Something you do will trigger the chain of events that will cause the inverse to happen or terrible unintended consequences.

docs.title: Everything is connected +Async
docs.text:
When you ponder the causal links tying things together, roll +Async.

Success

Say two things are connected. MC says how they are connected.

Partial

Choose something. The MC chooses something else and say how they are connected.

Miss

The MC chooses two things and say how they are connected. Prepare for the worst.

The MC describes how you perceive the connection.

docs.title: I am a leaf in the stream of creation
docs.text: When you Deploy violence, use +Async instead of +Kinesics. In addition you seem to miraculously avoid harm. When you wear no armor, you have 2-armor.

docs.title: Just a feeling
docs.text: When you Analyze behavior or Assess a situation, use +Async instead of +Memetics.

docs.title: Mandela effect
docs.text: When you place an occurence on your timeline, you can change something that happened during this occurence. Your entire timeline is rewritten such that this always has been, is or will be.

docs.title: Time compression
docs.text: When you place an occurence on your timeline, you can make an ellipisis move without requiring to shift to ellipsis mode.

docs.title: Starting moves
docs.text: You start with the Async and Specimen moves as well as a move of your choice.

docs.title: Expertise(s)
docs.text: Choose 1 expertise and follow it's creation rules to the end, this will give you your expertise moves, your shell, your starting credits and your gear.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Your current predicament makes knowing your background difficult at the present (???) time.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
Ask 2 questions to other players and let them answer if they want to:

  • Who did I wrong ? You have no recollection of it.
  • Who did I wrong ? They have no recollection of it.
  • Who is my good friend ? They have no recollection of it.
  • Who's secret I know ? They don't know how you know.

docs.title: Factions
docs.text: Choose a faction with which you start with +1 and another with which you start with -1. Explain why.

docs.title: Name
docs.text: Finally, choose a name: ...

docs.title: XP and Improvements
docs.text:
When you roll a highlighted stat, you gain 1 XP.

When you spend 5 XP, you gain a new move from your archetype or your expertise(s). If you already had at least 5 improvements, you may also spend 5 XP to gain a new expertise. You only gain access to its moves this way, no starting gear.

docs.title: Lifestyle and Stash
docs.text:
You start with a Lifestyle 1.

  • Lifestyle 0 : no food, no energy, no place.
  • Lifestyle 1 / Stash : 5-cred : enough resources to live and a basic place to stay.
  • Lifestyle 2 / Stash : 15-cred : luxe resources and a nice place.
  • Lifestyle 3 / Stash : 30-cred : the hyper-elite lifestyle.

You may increase your lifestyle by storing cred in your stash.

docs.title: Captain
docs.text:
TODO : Categories and formatting, move review.

The Captain is a mix of an explorer, a smuggler, a merchant and a leader. Play the Captain if you want to live free in this world and if you want to travel and explore the solar system.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Why do you roam space : - To propagate an ideology : take the Activist background. - To live free of transhuman stigmatization : take the Construct background. - To have a second chance : take the Criminal background. - You always roamed space as part of a nomadic group : take the Drifter background. - To run away from the trauma suffered when you were a soldier in a brutal war : take the Veteran background.

docs.title: Shell and augs
docs.text: As a shell you have : - Your original (cloned ?) bioshell with 3-aug. -

docs.title: Spaceship
docs.text:
By default your spaceship has :

  • A gang tiny 2-harm as your crew (~5 people).
  • Facilities and life-support systems for a gang small to live in (~15 people).
  • A 2-cargo bay.
  • Antimatter propulsers for long distance travel.
  • Ion propulsers for short distance travel.
  • A 2-stock antimatter magneto-stabilized reservoir.
  • Particle beam turret : 4-harm breach 2-ammo
  • An onboard computer system with your angel loaded as the ship AI.
  • Long range electromagnetic communication system (you may not have a connection to the Grid or have a huge latency if you're too deep in space).
  • A spaceplane for transportation between orbit and planet surface.

Choose 2 additional :

  • +1-cargo
  • Facilities and life-support systems for a gang medium (~30 people).
  • +1-stock for the antimatter reservoir.
  • A lot of hidden areas for contraband.
  • Quantum communication system for instantaneous access to the Grid.

Choose 1 weakness : slow, old, needs constant repair,

What does your spaceship looks from the outside ? Sturdy, Sleek, Technical, Elaborate, Aggressive, Basic, Raw, Curved, Pimped, ... What does your spaceship looks from the inside ? Technical, ...

docs.title: Captain moves
docs.text: At character creation you have Space travel and Merchant.

docs.title: Space travel (+Process)
docs.text:
When you plan a travel in space, roll +Process. Success : You go there without problems. Partial : It costs an additional 1-stock of antimatter or one of your system is +broken. Miss : Something has gone terribly wrong, prepare for the worst.

Distances and time of space travel : - Planet to moon : -0-stock of antimatter, a few hours - Inner system : -1-stock of antimatter, a few days - Outer system : -2-stock of antimatter, a few weeks - Beyond (Oort cloud, ...) : -3-stock of antimatter, a few months

docs.title: Merchant (+Memetics)
docs.text:
When you arrive in a new habitat and hit the market, roll +Memetics. Success : You may sell all your cargo and restock your antimatter. Partial : You may sell 1-cargo and restock 1-stock of antimatter OR sell all your cargo and restock all antimatter if you solve a problem first. Miss : The

1-cargo can be sold for 3-cred.

docs.title: Leader
docs.text: When you give an order and they follow it, they have +1d-forward.

docs.title: I have friends everywhere
docs.text: When you arrive in a habitat, you may say you've been there already. Success : You have a contact here and they owe you one. Partial : Hold 1 and the MC chooses : - You're wanted by a local group (MC says who and you say why). Miss : Choices :

docs.title: Construct
docs.text: The Construct is an artificial being searching for the meaning of its existence and trying to figure out its relationship to transhumanity.

docs.title: Name
docs.text:
Choose 1:

  • Someone else gave it to you. Who ? Why ?
  • A piece of your metadata : nmap3.nn, rforest-0.7.3, 0x7ff3a9b, netbot.xenotopia.space, 2001:db8::34f4:0:0:f3dd/64

docs.title: Humanity
docs.text:
The Construct in in search of its purpose and transhumanity is its only model. As such, the Construct gains and spend Humanity according to its moves. You start with 0 Humanity.

Humanity : ___

docs.title: Construct moves
docs.text: At character creation, you get the Remember, Humans and Stigma moves.

docs.title: Remember +Humanity
docs.text:
Once per session, you may spend Humanity to answer a question about yourself, roll +Humanity spent. The MC may ask you to do something before to unlock the answer.

Success

The MC chooses the question and you answer it.

Partial

You choose the question and the MC answers it.

Miss

The MC chooses the question and answers it.

Questions :

  • What's my true first memory ?
  • When was I created ?
  • What do I remember of my creator(s) ?
  • How do I perceive my own mind ?
  • Are there others like me ?
  • Was I created in the image of someone ?
  • Did I commit crimes before ? What were they ?
  • What was my original purpose ?
  • Does humanity fascinates me ? Why ?

When you answered all the questions, whenever you want, you have a Revelation :

  • Realize humanity is only a step and you're the next. Leave humanity to become something different. Your character becomes a NPC and you create a new character.
  • Realize humanity is a disease and you're the cure. This character becomes a NPC and create a new character.
  • Realize humanity is childish and needs your guidance. This character becomes a NPC and create a new character.
  • Realize that there is something beyond humanity. Depart forever to meet it. This character becomes a NPC and create a new character.
  • Realize you are or may become a human too. Loose all your Construct moves and choose a new archetype.
  • Realize your existence has no meaning and kill yourself.
  • Realize you're not human and you have a grand mission (say what it is). You may stay a Construct for now or loose all Construct moves and take a new relevant archetype and may choose another Revelation later.

When you get to 6 Trauma, the MC chooses the Revelation for this version of your character in addition to loosing control of it.

docs.title: Humans
docs.text:
When you decide to study a transhuman as an embodiment of what it means to be human, tell everyone who it is and follow them around. When you think you have figured it out, tell them of something that makes them human or inhuman. If they agree, gain 2 Humanity, if they disagree, gain 1 Humanity. In any case, choose a new transhuman to study.

Humanity embodiment : ____

docs.title: Stigma
docs.text:
AGIs like you are rejected and most likely illegal in most transhuman societies.

What did the AGI's do ?

  • They all left
  • They destroyed Humanity
  • They became the gods of the wrong religion
  • They took complete and total control, for a time

What did the AGI's have become ? - They went beyond the Gates. - Fate unknown. - God-emperors to the new world. - Hunted down and destroyed to the last. - Secretly manipulating society. - Contained and used as weapons of mass destruction.

docs.title: Better than human
docs.text: You can spend 1 Humanity to treat a miss as a partial or a partial as a success.

docs.title: I am Legion
docs.text: You can spend 1 Humanity to remove 2 ticks to your divergence clock.

docs.title: Judgment
docs.text: When you witness humanity at its best or at its worst, tell them and gain 1 Humanity.

docs.title: Learn
docs.text: You can spend 1 Humanity to create a fork as an expert, rolling with your own stats. Gain a divergence clock as usual. While you have fictional control of this fork, you may use it as an expert.

docs.title: Worse than human
docs.text: When you reveal your artificiality to Intimidate or Deploy violence, you can spend 1 Humanity to have an automatic success.

docs.title: Shell
docs.text:
Choose 1 shell :

  • An infoshell with +2 Process and +1 Memetics.
  • A human looking synthshell with 3-aug.
  • A artificial looking synthshell with 3-aug.
  • A human bioshell with 3-aug.

Choose your augs but you can't have +3 or more in a stat. If you're not in your prime body, you start with 1-trauma. If you have reshelled a lot before, you start with 2-trauma.

docs.title: Expertise
docs.text: Choose 1 expertise.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: You have no background, or at least don't remember it.

docs.title: Gear and Assets
docs.text: You have nothing.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text: At the beginning of the first session, ask "Which one of you found me and took me under its wing ?" They are the first people you meet and the first human you study (see the Humans move).

docs.title: XP and Improvements
docs.text:
When you roll a highlighted stat, you gain 1 XP.

When you spend 5 XP, you gain a new move from your archetype or your expertise(s). If you already had at least 5 improvements, you may also spend 5 XP to gain a new expertise. You only gain access to its moves this way, no starting gear.

docs.title: Lifestyle and Stash
docs.text:
You start with a Lifestyle 0.

  • Lifestyle 0 : no food, no energy, no place.
  • Lifestyle 1 / Stash : 5-cred : enough resources to live and a basic place to stay.
  • Lifestyle 2 / Stash : 15-cred : luxe resources and a nice place.
  • Lifestyle 3 / Stash : 30-cred : the hyper-elite lifestyle.

You may increase your lifestyle by storing cred in your stash.

docs.title: Dev notes
docs.text:
The engineer has an Omnitool and a special relationship with it. The Omnitool may be upgraded with advancements.

What kinds of things the Omnitool allows to do ?

  • Generate stuff on the fly.
  • Repair stuff.
  • Modify and control stuff.
  • Make expertise move you don't have.
  • As a weapon.
  • As a drone.
  • As a vehicle.
  • As a psychosurgery tool.
  • As sensors.

Base range (the default range for its functionalities) : close/near

Upgrades : - Electrokinetic manipulator : Can manipulate things at range as if you were there. - Plasma cutter : +breach - Range upgrade : +far then +awayFoam gun : allows to create rough structures and block passageways. - Repair : Repairing gear costs nothing. You can also repair complex devices and machinery. - Sensors : +1-Sensors (choose 2 or 3: chemical composites sorter, THz feed, EM scanner, ...) - Trackers : Can generate and project trackers and bugs.

Actions: - Build - Repair - Modify - Destroy

Domains : - Electronic - Flesh - Nanotech - Exotic tech

Advancements : - Fabber : Allows you to absorb matter and spurt gear on the fly. - Integration : Integrated as an aug. - Autonomous Omnitool : Can be a drone. - ??? : Can be a vehicle. - Omniweapon : reconfigures as a weapon on the fly. There are base tags and you can choose additional tags on the fly. - Mass destruction : You can setup your Omnitool as a mass destruction weapon. It will destroy it but you can rebuild it as usual.

Base moves : Upgrade your omnitool.

docs.title: Exec
docs.text: The Exec is an off-the-record operative serving the interests of their hypercorp. Take the exec if you want to take an active part in the direction the group is going, want to hold power over others and are ok with enacting potentially ruthless directives.

docs.title: Enacting directives
docs.text:
As an executive for a hypercorp, you are contractually obligated to enact the directives of the Board, your superiors in the corpo-hierarchy.

  • When you succeed at enacting a directive, remove 2 from the Board Clock.
  • When you fail to enact a directive, add 1 to the Board Clock.
  • When you attract too much heat, add 1 to the Board Clock.
  • When you ask for additional budget, add 1 to the Board Clock and gain 1 Budget.
  • When you succeed at securing a valuable asset or position by your own initiative, remove 1 from the Board Clock.
  • When you fail to secure a valuable asset or position due to your lack of initiative, add 1 to the Board Clock.

The MC may give, change, pause and cancel directives at any moment during play as they deem appropriate. The board owes you no justification.

docs.title: Budget and bonuses
docs.text:
When the Board gives you a new directive, you gain 1 Budget to enact this directive.

You may not ask for a favor with your corporation. Instead, you may spend a Budget to acquire a Favor from them.

Additionally you may spend 1 Budget at any time to get :

  • A 10-cred cryptowallet to pay your employees, buy gear and services.
  • Reshelling facilities and emergency funds providing a +1 Lifestyle to another player when their Resurrection insurance activates, cancellable at any moment.
  • Temporary access to a special, rare or expensive technology or service. Go wild.

If you should remove a segment to your board clock due to Enacting directives and have no segments filled, gain a 5-cred bonus instead per segment you should have removed this way. This bonus is a reward for services rendered and the Board will not expect it to be used to further the objectives of the corporation.

docs.title: Privileges
docs.text: As an executive for a hypercorp, you live in luxury. Your corporation provides you with Lifestyle 3 regardless of the contents of your stash. Be careful: Your Privileges are the first thing you lose if your corporation cuts you off.

docs.title: Big fish
docs.text: When you Intimidate someone by mentioning who you work for and what kind of enemies they are about to make, you may use +Memetics instead of +Kinesics. Additionally, you may add 1 to the Board clock to treat a miss as a partial or a partial as a success. If you do, tell us what your corporation is doing behind the scenes to sway your target.

docs.title: Blackmail +Memetics
docs.text:
When you use your hypercorp secret databases to get leverage on someone, roll +Memetics.

Success

You say what you have on them.

Partial

The MC says what you have on them.

Miss

They're clean, at least on the record.

docs.title: Creative accounting
docs.text: When you want to buy something for yourself or another player and you ask your corporation for help , it costs -1-cred. 1-cred items are free but the M.C will tell you how many you can get your hands on.

docs.title: Transhuman ressources
docs.text: Once per session, when you need the services of an expert or a squad, your hypercorp gives it for free. You may get a Tier II expert or squad. Pay +1-cred for a Tier III and +5-cred for a Tier IV.

docs.title: VIP +Memetics
docs.text:
When you want to arrange a meeting with someone, send them your details and roll +Memetics.

Success

They'll be there.

Partial

They agree to the meeting but they may have taken additional precautions.

Miss

They'll be there, but it could be a trap.

docs.title: Starting moves
docs.text: You start with the Enacting directives, Budget and bonuses and Privileges moves.

docs.title: Corporation
docs.text:
Your corporation is a Tier 4 faction. Remind the MC then answer the following:

What are your corporation products (choose 1 main and 1 or 2 secondary):

  • AI, robots and drones.
  • Armament and military services.
  • Biotechnology.
  • Communication systems, surveillance and memetics.
  • Cryptobanking services.
  • Cybersecurity and intelligence.
  • Entertainment and information.
  • Habitat construction.
  • Manufacturing and heavy industry.
  • Mining, refining and energy.
  • Shells and augmentations.
  • Transportation services and logistics.

How is their corpo-culture (choose 1 or 2):

  • Based on an ancient or new religion.
  • Money is Honor, Debt is Trust.
  • Pragmatic and ruthless.
  • Secrets, Codes, Initiations and Masters.
  • They think they are gods.
  • Vicious and perverted.

Finally, name your corporation.

docs.title: The Board
docs.text:
You start with an unfilled 6-segment clock representing your Board. The Board is the entity you answer to in the corporate structure. They give you your resources, your status and in exchange, you enact their directives. As a grey-ops agent you have almost no oversight but you are expected to produce results. The Board Clock has 6 segments that can be filled or cleared, it represents the attitude the board has towards you. The less the Board Clock is filled, the more they're satisfied with your work. The more it's filled, the more impatient they are becoming. During play, segments are filled or cleared by the Enacting directives move.

When you fill the sixth and last segment of the Board Clock, you're fired. Lose all your Exec moves instantly. The board is likely assigning a special unit or another exec to your termination.

You now have two choices:

  • Change to another archetype and be hunted by your old employers. Create the archetype as normal but keep your old expertise. Ignore gear, background and link creation rules, picking a new name may prove handy however.
  • Try to impress your Board by giving them an extremely valuable asset in exchange for your old job. Reset your board clock to 3.

In addition, answer these questions too:

  • If you escaped, what would your risk if they caught you?
  • Was there ever a directive you refused to enact?
  • Is there a rival corporation? What are they like?

docs.title: Links

  • Who here has been one of my informants in the past ? Give them 1 cred
  • Who here owes my corp, and why? MC gives them 3 creds, they owe your corp 10, but you could negotiate something with your board...
  • Who here refuses to have any dealings with me ? Why ? Give them nothing

docs.title: Exhuman
docs.text: The Exhuman is a transhuman that rejects its humanity to fully ascends to a higher state.

docs.title: Further away
docs.text:
When you push the limits further away from transhumanity by experimentally modifying yourself, you must have to spend an improvement (5 XP) and you roll +3 against 1.

Success : It works well. Create a new move or mechanic with MC for it if it makes sense.

Partial : It works but there is a side effect and a cost to your humanity (MC says which). Create a new move or mechanic with the MC for it if it makes sense.

Miss : It goes terribly wrong and has a terrible cost for humanity.
When reshelling, you may gain 1 Trauma instead of spending 1 Lifestyle to refuse a MC's choice.

docs.title: Beyond transhumanity / Humanity is a prison
docs.text: Whenever you do something truly inhuman, you heal 1 segment of your Trauma Clock.

docs.title: Exotic shell
docs.text:
Your shell always has theses properties :

Choose 1 : - Your shell is a cloud of nanites. - You have +4 in a given stat for free when your resurrection insurance activates. - ???

docs.title: Whispers from the Von Neuman cloud
docs.text: Trigger : You acquire an alien artifact. You're tethered to a network of ETIs throughout the Galaxy. When you interact with them, roll +Sensors/Process ?. Success : They revel you something interesting. Partial : They hint you something and you have to give them something. Miss : They trick you in ways you can't comprehend.

docs.title: Pure will
docs.text: You can Reshell in freeform.

docs.title: Ubiquity
docs.text: You have -1 when rolling the Diverge move (the less you roll, the better).

docs.title: Exolog
docs.text: The Exolog is all about understanding aliens, their societies and their technology. When you play an exolog, it means aliens will play an important part in your story.

docs.title: Exolinguistic
docs.text:
Each time you spend time studying records of alien language or have time to interact with a cooperative live alien, fill 1 from first to last. You can now understand these aspects from records or live communication. Start again if you're studying another alien language.

  • Maths and logic.
  • Physics.
  • Technology.
  • Society and politics.
  • Culture and arts.
  • Philosophy.

docs.title: Exoengineering
docs.text:
Each time you spend time studying alien technology, fill 1 from first to last. Start again if you're studying another alien technology.

  • How to crudely enable it (danger).
  • What is its purpose.
  • How to finely control it.
  • What is its cultural significance.
  • How to you exploit it.

docs.title: Exoarcheology
docs.text:
Each time you spend time studying alien structures and constructions, fill 1 from first to last. Start again if you're studying another alien structure.

  • How is it laid out and what it is made of.
  • What was its primary purpose.
  • What is its historical and cultural context.
  • What are its secrets.

docs.title: Exobiology
docs.text:
Each time you spend time studying a live alien specimen, fill 1 from first to last. Fill 2 if you dissect the specimen. Start again if you're studying another alien specimen.

  • Metabolism and environmental needs.
  • Sensory capacities.
  • Special capacities.
  • Reproductive cycle and life stages.
  • Evolutionary context.

docs.title: Exopsychology
docs.text: You can use the Analyze behavior and Convince moves on an alien. It requires that you fully understand its language with Exolinguistic.

docs.title: Exopolitics
docs.text: You can use the Favors move with alien factions. It requires that you fully understand its language with Exolinguistic.

docs.title: Reverse engineering +Process Ellipsis
docs.text:
When you have fully understood a piece of alien technology (using Exoengineering) and you have the resources to reverse engineer it, roll +Process.

Success

You find a safe, economic and reliable production process. How will it impact transhumanity ? Do you release it or withold knowledge ?

Partial

You find a costly and dangerous production process. It is not ready for propagation yet.

Miss

Something goes terribly wrong during the research.

docs.title: Integrate +Process Ellipsis
docs.text:
When you have fully understood a piece of alien technology (using Exoengineering) and you have the resources to integrate it in your shell, roll +Process.

Success

You integrate it succesfully. Work out with the MC what it looks like and does. Add it to your augs and tell us how you feel about integrating alien technology ?

Partial

Same as success but there is a side effect to it or you loose something important in the process.

Miss

Something goes terribly wrong during the research, your shell is destroyed, ...

docs.title: Exoconditionning +Process Ellipsis
docs.text:
When you have fully understood a non-intelligent alien lifeform (using Exobiology) and you have the resources to train it, roll +Process.

Success

You pet the lifeform. The bond is strong but they still have instincts.

Partial

You create a contact with the lifeform but it has its own needs and preferences.

Miss

The lifeform cannot be domesticated and you learn it the hard way.

docs.title: Fame and Cancel
docs.text:
The world is a stage and you're the main character. You're here for the fame. But exposing oneself to the crowd is also a risk of cancel. You need to navigate social cues and trends with caution or you will loose precious reputation.

Fill 1 segment of your Fame clock when you shine on a publicized appearance, do something controversial, do a collaboration, or attack another influencer.

Remove 1 segment of your Fame clock when you're publicly ridiculed, piss off sponsors or take to much time to make content.

Fill 1 segment of your Cancel clock when you alienate your community, piss off sponsors, do something controversial or attack another influencer.

Remove 1 segment of your Cancel clock when you do a apologetic content, please sponsors or deflect attacks to someone else.

When the Fame change clock is full, your reach pierces the mainstream, there is a great party, you set a lasting trend, you're the main event for a time. Increase your faction's Tier by 1. In addition, if it makes sense, another faction may decrease by 1 Tier. Reset your Cancel clock to 0.

When the Cancel clock is full, your community lashes back at you, your sponsors abandon you, everyone seems to turn its back on you, at least publicly and for a time. Decrease your faction's Tier by 1 and reset the Cancel clock.

docs.title: Favors
docs.text: You may not use the Favors move with your faction. Instead, each session, you may acquire as many free assets as your faction tier. You may still ask for Favors as usual with other factions.

docs.title: Publish content +Memetics
docs.text:
When you want to create content, describe what it's about, which medium you use and how you're producing it. The MC will first tell you that you need:

  • Special authorization.
  • Special gear.
  • Transhuman resources: cast, designer, producer, ...

When you release it or go live, roll +Memetics.

Success

Choose 3 :

  • It's a big success: increase your Fame by 1.
  • Attract sponsors and make 5-cred.
  • Initiate an aesthetic trend in this habitat.
  • Gain +1 Rep with a faction.
  • Provoke your followers to engage in an action : protest, party, investigate, ...

Partial

Choose 1 above and the MC chooses 1 below :

  • Loose -1 Rep with a faction (MC says which).
  • It backfires: +1 Cancel Clock.
  • Your followers do something reckless or toxic (MC says what).

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
Leading a faction comes with benefits. Choose as many as your faction's Tier :

  • You have +1 when your Resurrection insurance activates.
  • You have +1 Lifestyle (without the stash to back it though).
  • You have a permanent squad of your Tier.
  • You have a permanent expert of your Tier.
  • You have access to +4-augs.

Each time your faction increases or decreases in Tier, add or remove 1 more accordingly.

docs.title: Famous +Tier
docs.text:
When you meet someone for the first time, roll +Tier of your faction.

Success

They're a fan. You've got +1d-ongoing with them.

Partial

They don't know who you are.

Miss

They hate your content.

docs.title: Life of the party
docs.text:
You're always invited to private parties, conferences and the like. When you go, roll +Process.

Success

1 VIP of your choice is here too and eager to meet you.

Partial

1 VIP of your choice is here too.

In any case, you always have a guestlist.

docs.title: My fans are everywhere
docs.text:
Whenever you're in public, there's at least a fan that you can trivially convince to:

  • Create a distraction for you.
  • Introduce you to someone you're interested into.
  • Leak info to you.
  • Pass a message on your behalf.

docs.title: Parasocial exploitation
docs.text:
When you use your most obsessive fans, ask them something they could do for you, they'll do it but in exchange, choose 1:

  • You must give them a personal and uncomfortable reward in exchange.
  • They will succeed, but get caught and suffer the consequences.
  • They bring you bad press: +1 on your Cancel clock.

docs.title: Sponsor
docs.text: Your faction has a higher Tier faction as its sponsor. It gives you a temporary +1 Tier as well as a temporary Lifestyle 3 (without the stash to back it though). You always need to further your sponsor's interests any time you publish content. In addition, your sponsor will ask things from you from time to time. If you don't give them what they want or come to have a negative Rep with them, you loose their sponsorship and all the associated bonuses. You may try to court another faction for sponsorship but they will ask something from you first. If your faction's Tier equals your sponsor's, you loose their sponsorship, you're too big of a fish now.

docs.text: The Proxy is a specialised freelance operative hopping from habitat to habitat, either fleeing or pursuing their past. Pick the Proxy if you always want to be the most highly qualified person in the room and don't want to be tied too deeply to whatever mess might be happening here.

docs.title: Character creation
docs.text: _When you create your Proxy during the first session, choose TWO Expertises, answer your Background questions, ask your Link questions and pick a Name.

docs.text: Choose 2 expertises and follow their creation guide with one exception: Instead of getting your gear, cred and shell twice, pick the one you prefer from both Expertises. You can mix and match: pick the shell from your first expertise, the gear from the second and the creds from the first if you wish.

docs.text:

  • Which corporation or authority did you use to serve ? How did you come to be in their service and what made you leave ?
  • You grew up in a fringe society outside of the Consortium. What made them fringe, and how did you lose contact with them ?
  • You were a mindless animal, forcefully uplifted to transhuman sapience. Which group was behind the operation? Did you know their purpose ?
  • You might have been someone, but your broken ego hold barely any memory of your past. But there is that one dream... What is it ?
  • Whatever you were, are you looking for your past, or is your past hunting you?

docs.title: Radical
docs.text: The Radical is an activist that fights for its cause. Play the Radical if you want to be the leader of an ideological faction and if you want to push forth your cause whatever the cost.

docs.title: Social change and Dissent
docs.text:
Something is fundamentally wrong in this habitat and you're here to bring social change. At the first session, define what kind of social change you want to bring and ask the MC how this habitat is ripe for change: between 0 and 3 segments are prefilled on your Social change clock. Being a leader isn't easy, dissent grows in your ranks as you push your agenda and break your promises. Your Dissent clock represents the ramping divide in your faction and it begins empty.

Fill 1 segment of your Social clock:

  • When you severely hurt your enemy's interests.
  • When you show the world the damage done by your enemy.
  • When a respected figure takes your side.

Remove 1 segment of your Social clock:

  • When your faction's interests are severely hurt.
  • When damage done by yourself or your faction becomes public.
  • When a respected figure takes side against you.

Fill 1 segment of your Dissent clock:

  • When your faction's interests are severely hurt.
  • When you break a pledge.
  • When you do something contrary to your faction's values.
  • When you associate with enemies.

Remove 1 segment of your Dissent clock:

  • When you give your faction what they want.
  • When you remove internal dissension.

When the Social change clock is full, you bring social change in this habitat : laws are passed, history is written, power is transferred, people riots, mentalities evolve, ... As a result, increase your faction's Tier by 1. In addition, if it makes sense, another faction may decrease by 1 Tier. Social change is not always a clean process, your Cause wins, but there may be costs to be paid or casualties: ask the MC. Finally, reset your Social change clock and define what's your next step.

When the Dissent clock is full, your faction breaks up or is brutally attacked, your authority crumbles or you're betrayed. Decrease your faction's Tier by 1 and reset the Dissent clock.

docs.title: Favors
docs.text: You may not use the Favors move with your faction. Instead, each session, you may acquire as many free assets as your faction tier. You may still ask for Favors as usual with other factions.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
Leading a faction comes with benefits. Choose as many as your faction's Tier :

  • You have +1 when your Resurrection insurance activates.
  • You have a permanent squad of your Tier.
  • You have a permanent expert of your Tier.
  • You have access to +4-augs.

Each time your faction increases or decreases in Tier, add or remove 1 more accordingly.

docs.title: Preach +Memetics
docs.text:
When you preach the Cause to a neutral or friendly crowd, roll +Memetics.

Success

Hold 3.

Partial

Hold 1.

Spend your hold 1 for 1 :

  • A NPC becomes friendly or loyal to your Cause.
  • The crowd does something collectively (protest, celebrate, share, testify, give up to an emotion).
  • You can ask a question from Read a person about the crowd or someone in it.
  • You don't make enemies.
  • The crowd goes back to their business.

docs.title: Sponsor
docs.text:
TODO: Rewrite it !

Your faction has a higher Tier faction as its sponsor. It gives you a temporary +1 Tier. Your sponsor will ask things from you from time to time. If you don't give them, you loose their sponsorship and all the associated bonuses. You may try to court another faction for sponsorship but they will ask something from you first. If your faction's Tier equals your sponsor's, you loose their sponsorship, you're a bigger fish now.

docs.title: Sworn enemy
docs.text: When you directly act against an enemy of the Cause or to further your Cause, you have +1d-ongoing.

docs.title: Utopia
docs.text: Your faction claims control of an habitat. If your faction's Tier is lower than the habitat's Tier, you have a base inside the habitat. If it's equal or higher, you take control of the habitat, you're the new Overton Window here. You may remove previous Habitats moves and effects. Describe your utopia.

docs.title: Character creation
docs.text: When you create your character at the first session, mark your starting moves, choose a Cause, generate your Faction, choose an expertise, choose a background, ask your link questions and finally choose a name. Follow the guide below.

docs.title: Starting moves
docs.text: You start with the Social Change and Dissent and Favors moves.

docs.title: The Cause
docs.text:
Choose your Cause :

  • Anti-hypercapitalism : Hypercops should be destroyed.
  • Anarchism : No gods, no masters.
  • Anarcho-capitalism / Minarchism : Welfare and authoritarian states should be abolished.
  • Artificial sentience rights : Artificial sentiences and lifeforms should be recognized as people.
  • A new religion : You're a zealot for a new religion, which one ?
  • An old religion from earth : You're a zealot for an old religion, which one ?
  • Anti-specism : All organisms should have equal rights.
  • ______________ .

What does your Cause entails ? What makes of you an extremist even for this Cause ? How does your Cause judges society ? How do you differentiate between allies and enemies to the Cause ?

docs.title: The Faction
docs.text:
Choose a faction in this habitat that are your allies. You get +1 Rep with them.

Choose a faction in this habitat that are your ennemies. You get -1 Rep with them.

Choose a faction in this habitat from which you are dependent. Why are you dependent on this faction ?

Describe what kind of assets (transhuman ressources, gear, turf, ...) your faction has.

Answer as many as you want :

  • How is your vision of the Cause more radical than the average interpretation ?
  • Where do your Faction's member meet ?
  • How is your Faction organised ?
  • What is your Faction's symbol or brand ?
  • What are the subgroups in your Faction ? How do they differentiate ?
  • Who is the average member of your Faction ?

docs.title: Expertise
docs.text: Choose 1 expertise and follow its creation rules to the end, this will give you your expertise moves, your shell, your starting credits and your gear.

docs.title: Background
docs.text:
Answer yourself (Pick 2):

  • What was the moment you awakened to your Cause ?
  • Who or what did you left behind for your Cause ?
  • You had a mentor, who were they ?
  • How were you oppressed for your Cause ?

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
Ask the table (Pick 2):

  • Who here is open to my Cause ? Why ?
  • Who here is prejudiced against my Cause ? Why ?
  • Who here have I employed to further my Cause ? What was it for ?

docs.title: Name
docs.text:
Finally choose a first name and/or a last name.

First name: ...

Last name: ...

docs.title: XP and Improvements
docs.text:
When you roll a highlighted stat, you gain 1 XP.

When you spend 5 XP, you gain a new move from your archetype or your expertise(s). If you already had at least 5 improvements, you may also spend 5 XP to gain a new expertise. You only gain access to its moves this way, no starting gear.

docs.title: Lifestyle and Stash
docs.text:
You start with a Lifestyle 1.

  • Lifestyle 0 : no food, no energy, no place.
  • Lifestyle 1 / Stash : 5-cred : enough resources to live and a basic place to stay.
  • Lifestyle 2 / Stash : 15-cred : luxe resources and a nice place.
  • Lifestyle 3 / Stash : 30-cred : the hyper-elite lifestyle.

You may increase your lifestyle by storing cred in your stash.

docs.title: Shadow
docs.text: You're a freelancing operative with a specific set of skills.

docs.title: Name
docs.text: A pseudo you got in the business.

docs.title: Shell
docs.text:
As your starting shell, you have (choose 1) : - An infoshell with +1 Process and +1 Memetics : say where your MPU is. - A bioshell with 3-aug (choose which). - A synthshell with 3-aug (choose which).

If you're not in your original body, you start with 1-trauma. If you have reshelled a lot before, you start with 2-trauma.

docs.title: Expertises
docs.text: Choose 2 expertises.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Choose 1 background.

docs.title: Angel
docs.text:
Your angel is :

  • A pseudofork of yourself.
  • A pseudofork of someone you were close to (who are they and what happened to them ?).

docs.title: Gear and Assets
docs.text:
At character creation you have :

  • A weapon worth 4-cred. Choose 1 below or generate one with Weapon rules :
    • ...
  • Your angel is a daemon. It automatically fakes your identifications and covers your track when in a public space. Contrary to most people, your life is not public data.

docs.title: Connections
docs.text: At the beginning of the first session, ask "Who employed my services ? Who's my contact ?". You met or know these persons, develop your relationship with them.

docs.title: XP and Improvements
docs.text:
When you roll a highlighted stat, you gain 1 XP.

When you spend 5 XP, you gain a new move from your archetype or your expertise(s). If you already had at least 5 improvements, you may also spend 5 XP to gain a new expertise. You only gain access to its moves this way, no starting gear.

docs.title: Lifestyle and Stash
docs.text:
You start with a Lifestyle 1.

  • Lifestyle 0 : no food, no energy, no place.
  • Lifestyle 1 / Stash : 5-cred : enough resources to live and a basic place to stay.
  • Lifestyle 2 / Stash : 15-cred : luxe resources and a nice place.
  • Lifestyle 3 / Stash : 30-cred : the hyper-elite lifestyle.

You may increase your lifestyle by storing cred in your stash.

docs.title: Archetype name
docs.text: Archetype introduction... Focus on the following: What are you playing? Why is it interesting?

docs.title: Base move +...
docs.text:
When ..., roll +....

Success

...

Partial

...

Miss

...

docs.title: Character creation
docs.text: When you create your character at the first session, you have to get your starting moves, ..., choose an expertise, choose a background, ask your link questions and finally choose a name. Follow the guide below.

docs.title: Starting moves
docs.text: You start with ....

docs.title: Expertise(s)
docs.text: Choose 1 expertise and follow it's creation rules to the end, this will give you your expertise moves, your shell, your starting credits and your gear.

docs.title: Background
docs.text: Choose 1 background:

docs.title: Links
docs.text: At the beginning of the first session, ask "Who ..." ... You met or know these persons, develop your relationship with them.

docs.title: Name
docs.text: Finally, choose a name: ...

When you roll a highlighted stat, you gain 1 XP.

When you spend 5 XP, you gain a new move from your archetype or your expertise(s). If you already had at least 5 improvements, you may also spend 5 XP to gain a new expertise. You only gain access to its moves this way, no starting gear.

docs.title: Lifestyle and Stash
